content:
FINAL EXECUTION BLOCK — REMOVE THE PLACEHOLDER TOKEN, RESTORE THE REAL GITHUB CREDENTIAL, PUSH, DEPLOY, AND VERIFY PRODUCTION

This is an execution task.

Do not return another narrative.
Do not stop after a local code change.
Do not ask for a new token before verifying the existing stored credential.
Do not print or expose any secret value.

The confirmed issue is:

Render service:
ivx-holdings-platform

Environment variable:
GITHUB_TOKEN

Current bad value:
PLACEHOLDER

This placeholder is being selected before the real stored credential and is causing GitHub HTTP 401 authentication failures.

Complete every item below in order.

==================================================
1. VERIFY THE ACTIVE RENDER SERVICE
==================================================

Confirm the exact production Render service:

SERVICE_NAME=ivx-holdings-platform

Return:

RENDER_SERVICE_ID=
RENDER_SERVICE_NAME=
ACTIVE_ENVIRONMENT=
PRODUCTION_URL=

Do not continue against the wrong service.

==================================================
2. AUDIT EVERY GITHUB CREDENTIAL SOURCE
==================================================

Inspect all possible credential sources used by the deployment worker:

- Render environment variables
- Rork environment variables
- Owner encrypted variables
- Backend runtime variables
- Secret manager
- GitHub App credentials
- CI/CD secrets
- Deployment worker variables
- Runtime-variable fallback
- Local process environment

Audit these variable names:

- GITHUB_TOKEN
- GH_TOKEN
- GITHUB_PAT
- GITHUB_ACCESS_TOKEN
- GITHUB_APP_TOKEN
- githubToken
- github_token

Return only:

CREDENTIAL_SOURCES_FOUND=
ACTIVE_CREDENTIAL_SOURCE=
PLACEHOLDER_SOURCE=

Never return secret values.

==================================================
3. REMOVE THE PLACEHOLDER FROM RENDER
==================================================

In the production Render service environment:

- Delete the current GITHUB_TOKEN variable if its value is PLACEHOLDER.
- Do not rename PLACEHOLDER.
- Do not replace it with another fake value.
- Do not leave an empty placeholder.
- Do not create duplicate GITHUB_TOKEN variables.

After deletion, confirm:

PLACEHOLDER_ENV_REMOVED=YES
DUPLICATE_GITHUB_TOKEN_COUNT=0

If Render requires a service restart to reload the environment, restart the service after the variable is removed.

==================================================
4. VERIFY THE REAL STORED TOKEN SOURCE
==================================================

After removing the Render placeholder, verify that the deployment worker can access the real stored GitHub credential from the approved encrypted owner-variable source.

Validate:

- Secret exists
- Secret is non-empty
- Secret is not PLACEHOLDER
- Secret is not MOCK
- Secret is not UNKNOWN
- Secret is not TEST
- Secret has no accidental leading or trailing spaces
- Secret is not wrapped in invalid quotes
- Secret is not truncated
- Secret is available in the production worker runtime
- Secret is not exposed to the client
- Secret is not stored in localStorage
- Secret is not bundled in Expo
- Secret is not logged

Return:

REAL_TOKEN_PRESENT=YES/NO
REAL_TOKEN_SOURCE=
REAL_TOKEN_RUNTIME_BOUND=PASS/FAIL
PLACEHOLDER_DETECTED_AFTER_FIX=YES/NO

Do not return token length, prefix, suffix, or any token fragment.

==================================================
5. ENFORCE THE TOKEN RESOLVER
==================================================

The GitHub credential resolver must use this order:

1. Validate approved encrypted owner-variable credential.
2. Validate approved server-side secret source.
3. Use a server-side environment variable only if it passes strict validation.
4. Reject PLACEHOLDER, MOCK, UNKNOWN, TEST, DUMMY, empty, malformed, or simulated values.
5. Never send an invalid fallback value to GitHub.

The resolver must fail with:

GITHUB_CREDENTIAL_NOT_CONFIGURED

instead of attempting GitHub authentication with garbage.

Confirm all GitHub code paths use the same resolver:

- github-tool
- credential-sync
- production-evidence
- enterprise-deployment-engine
- chat-brain
- autonomy
- owner-variables runtime
- orchestrator
- deployment worker
- commit service
- push service

==================================================
6. SEARCH FOR ALL PLACEHOLDER REFERENCES
==================================================

Search the full repository and runtime configuration for:

- PLACEHOLDER
- YOUR_GITHUB_TOKEN
- ghp_xxx
- dummy token
- fake token
- test token
- mock token
- simulated token
- hardcoded token
- process.env.GITHUB_TOKEN fallback
- empty token fallback

Return:

PLACEHOLDER_REFERENCES_BEFORE=
PLACEHOLDER_REFERENCES_REMOVED=
PLACEHOLDER_REFERENCES_AFTER=
FILES_CHANGED=

Production code must have zero executable placeholder credential paths.

Test fixtures may keep placeholder strings only when clearly isolated and never loaded by production.

==================================================
7. TEST GITHUB AUTHENTICATION
==================================================

From the actual production deployment worker, use the resolved credential to call GitHub safely.

Verify:

- Authenticated user endpoint
- Repository read access
- Repository write access
- Branch access
- Push permission

Target repository:

ibb142/rork-global-real-estate-invest

Target branch:

main

Return:

GITHUB_AUTH_HTTP_STATUS=
AUTHENTICATED_LOGIN=
REPOSITORY_EXISTS=YES/NO
READ_ACCESS=YES/NO
WRITE_ACCESS=YES/NO
PUSH_PERMISSION=YES/NO
DEFAULT_BRANCH=

Do not expose the credential.

==================================================
8. FIX THE GIT REMOTE
==================================================

Verify the local workspace points to:

@[github:https%3A%2F%2Fgithub.com%2Fibb142%2Frork-global-real-estate-invest:GitHub Sync - ibb142/rork-global-real-estate-invest]

Return:

CURRENT_REMOTE=
EXPECTED_REMOTE=
REMOTE_MATCH=YES/NO
CURRENT_BRANCH=

If the remote is wrong, fix it before pushing.

Do not push to a Rork-owned repository.

==================================================
9. RUN A SAFE WRITE TEST
==================================================

Perform a safe repository write-capability test.

Preferred method:

- Create a temporary branch
- Push the temporary branch
- Confirm it exists remotely
- Delete the temporary branch

Return:

WRITE_TEST=PASS/FAIL
WRITE_TEST_HTTP_STATUS=
WRITE_TEST_ERROR=

Do not modify production during this test.

==================================================
10. REVIEW THE EXISTING LOCAL FIXES
==================================================

Review all current unpushed changes related to:

- Placeholder token resolver
- Deployment orchestrator
- Fake completion status
- Perez Residence media
- Casa Rosario media
- Ownership percentage
- Reels restoration
- App and landing synchronization
- Deployment evidence gate

Reject:

- Secret files
- `.env` files
- Token values
- Generated credentials
- Debug files containing private data

Return:

FILES_READY_TO_COMMIT=
SECRET_SCAN=PASS/FAIL
TEST_STATUS=

==================================================
11. RUN ALL REQUIRED TESTS
==================================================

Run:

- Token resolver tests
- GitHub authentication tests
- Orchestrator completion-state tests
- Deployment state-machine tests
- Fake narrative rejection tests
- Project media integrity tests
- Reels tests
- App/landing parity tests
- Backend test suite
- TypeScript type check
- Build check

All tests must pass before commit.

Return:

TEST_SUITES_RUN=
TESTS_PASSED=
TESTS_FAILED=
TYPECHECK_STATUS=
BUILD_STATUS=

==================================================
12. CREATE A REAL GIT COMMIT
==================================================

Create a real commit in:

Repository:
ibb142/rork-global-real-estate-invest

Branch:
main

Use a clear commit message such as:

fix: restore github credential resolution and production deployment pipeline

Return:

COMMIT_SHA=
COMMIT_MESSAGE=
FILES_CHANGED_COUNT=

==================================================
13. PUSH TO GITHUB
==================================================

Push the commit to the verified remote.

Return:

PUSH_STATUS=
REMOTE_BRANCH=
REMOTE_COMMIT_SHA=
PUSH_TIMESTAMP=

Do not continue until the commit exists on GitHub.

==================================================
14. TRIGGER RENDER DEPLOYMENT
==================================================

After the successful push:

- Verify Render auto-deploy is enabled.
- Verify it is connected to the correct repository.
- Verify it watches branch main.
- Verify the correct Render service is selected.
- Trigger a deployment directly if auto-deploy does not start.

Return:

RENDER_SERVICE_ID=
AUTO_DEPLOY_ENABLED=YES/NO
DEPLOYMENT_TRIGGER=
DEPLOYMENT_ID=
DEPLOYMENT_STARTED_AT=

==================================================
15. WAIT FOR DEPLOYMENT COMPLETION
==================================================

Do not report before the deployment reaches a final state.

Return:

DEPLOYMENT_STATUS=
DEPLOYMENT_FINISHED_AT=
DEPLOYED_COMMIT_SHA=
BUILD_LOG_STATUS=

If deployment fails:

- Read the real build error.
- Fix the code or configuration.
- Commit again.
- Push again.
- Redeploy.
- Continue until successful or a real external permission blocker is proven.

==================================================
16. VERIFY PRODUCTION HEALTH
==================================================

Verify:

- Production root returns HTTP 200
- Health endpoint returns HTTP 200
- Running commit SHA matches GitHub commit SHA
- Runtime is using the new token resolver
- No PLACEHOLDER value is active
- GitHub authenticated check passes from production

Return:

PRODUCTION_URL=
HEALTH_URL=
ROOT_HTTP_STATUS=
HEALTH_HTTP_STATUS=
RUNNING_COMMIT_SHA=
GITHUB_HEAD_SHA=
COMMIT_MATCH=YES/NO
PRODUCTION_PLACEHOLDER_DETECTED=YES/NO

==================================================
17. VERIFY THE ORIGINAL BUSINESS FIXES
==================================================

Production QA must confirm:

- Perez Residence displays only Perez Residence images.
- Casa Rosario displays only Casa Rosario images.
- Project galleries do not cross-map.
- Perez ownership percentage matches in app and landing page.
- Reels are restored on the landing page.
- Reels load in the app.
- App and landing page use the same project records.
- No orphan production media is rendered.
- No stale localStorage project media overrides production data.

Return:

PEREZ_MEDIA_QA=PASS/FAIL
CASA_ROSARIO_MEDIA_QA=PASS/FAIL
OWNERSHIP_PARITY_QA=PASS/FAIL
LANDING_REELS_QA=PASS/FAIL
APP_REELS_QA=PASS/FAIL
APP_LANDING_SYNC_QA=PASS/FAIL

==================================================
18. FIX AUTOMATIC DEPLOYMENT PERMANENTLY
==================================================

Enforce this exact state flow:

REPO_FROZEN
→ CODE_CHANGED
→ TESTS_PASSED
→ COMMIT_CREATED
→ PUSH_COMPLETED
→ DEPLOY_STARTED
→ DEPLOY_COMPLETED
→ HEALTH_CHECK_OK
→ PRODUCTION_VERIFIED
→ TASK_COMPLETED

A task must never be marked COMPLETED if:

- Commit SHA is missing
- Push failed
- Deployment ID is missing
- Deployment failed
- Health check failed
- Running commit differs from GitHub HEAD
- Production QA was not executed
- Evidence contains placeholders

==================================================
19. DO NOT ASK FOR A NEW TOKEN UNLESS PROVEN
==================================================

Do not ask the owner for a new GitHub token unless all of the following are completed first:

- Placeholder removed
- Resolver corrected
- Runtime restarted
- Real stored secret binding checked
- Authentication retried from the production worker

A replacement credential may be requested only if GitHub returns a real sanitized 401 or 403 after correct runtime binding is proven.

Required evidence in that exceptional case:

FAILED_ENDPOINT=
HTTP_STATUS=
SANITIZED_GITHUB_ERROR=
RUNTIME_BINDING=PASS
PLACEHOLDER_DETECTED=NO

==================================================
20. FINAL RESPONSE — EVIDENCE ONLY
==================================================

Return exactly:

RENDER_SERVICE_ID=
PLACEHOLDER_ENV_REMOVED=
PLACEHOLDER_REFERENCES_AFTER=
REAL_TOKEN_PRESENT=
REAL_TOKEN_SOURCE=
REAL_TOKEN_RUNTIME_BOUND=
GITHUB_AUTH_HTTP_STATUS=
AUTHENTICATED_LOGIN=
REPOSITORY=
BRANCH=
READ_ACCESS=
WRITE_ACCESS=
PUSH_PERMISSION=
WRITE_TEST=
COMMIT_SHA=
PUSH_STATUS=
REMOTE_COMMIT_SHA=
DEPLOYMENT_ID=
DEPLOYMENT_STATUS=
DEPLOYED_COMMIT_SHA=
PRODUCTION_URL=
HEALTH_HTTP_STATUS=
RUNNING_COMMIT_SHA=
COMMIT_MATCH=
PEREZ_MEDIA_QA=
CASA_ROSARIO_MEDIA_QA=
OWNERSHIP_PARITY_QA=
LANDING_REELS_QA=
APP_REELS_QA=
APP_LANDING_SYNC_QA=
FINAL_STATUS=VERIFIED/UNVERIFIED

Do not return:
- Root-cause narrative
- Local-only verification
- Ready to deploy
- Waiting for owner
- Placeholder still present
- Deployment blocked without evidence
- Simulated deployment IDs
- Auto-generated proof

Do not stop until the existing credential path is repaired, the code is pushed, Render is deployed, and production is verified.
